## Key Decisions
- Wave 2 sizes: types 155 + grid-planner 148 + tmux-multiplexer 553 + session-manager 423 = 1,279 new LOC total (Wave 1+2 combined)
- tmux-multiplexer.ts is 553 LOC (vs ~250 target) because of per-method JSDoc + per-method ORIGIN annotations — acceptable
- **`EnrichedSessionEvent` actual shape**: `{ type: "session.created", properties: { info: { id, parentID, title, directory } }, hivemindMeta?: { agent, delegationId, depth } }` — read from `event.properties.info.id`/`directory`/`title` + `event.hivemindMeta.agent`/`delegationId`/`depth`
- **`ForkSessionManager` interface declares ONLY `onSessionCreated`** (observers.ts:37-39)
- In-tree `SessionManager.respawnIfKnown` returns `Promise<{ paneId: string } | null>` (NOT `Promise<boolean>`) — returns `null` if not tracked / tmux unavailable / spawn failed; returns `{paneId: tracked.paneId}` on success (existing or freshly-spawned)
- integration.ts bridge: `setSessionManagerAdapter(adapter)` called in Step 9 (after Step 8 builds the adapter) publishes to module-level slot in types.ts. Replaces fork-bridge's `setForkSessionManager`. Replace-only semantics (matches HMR-safe behavior). `getSessionManagerAdapter()` consumed by `tmux-copilot.ts` at execute() time
- `plugin.ts` observer wiring simplified: `tmuxIntegration.adapter` is passed directly to `createTmuxEventObserver` (no bridge lookup needed at plugin-init time, since factory already returned the adapter) — structural typing satisfies `ForkSessionManager` constraint
- `TmuxIntegration` interface (new in integration.ts) exposes `multiplexer` + `sessionManager_` + `adapter` for future plugin-time wiring (observer hookup, telemetry)
- integration.ts Step 2 checks `process.env.TMUX` (the canonical signal that we're running inside a tmux session) — combined with tmux binary check, the factory is "inside tmux AND tmux is on PATH" — silent null if either fails
- `readOrMigratePort` (renamed from `readPersistedPort`): the function does NOT return `null` on cold start — it always returns a port via deterministic SHA-256 hash fallback (10000..65535 range). New name documents the "persist or derive" behavior. Returns `null` only on malformed persisted file (parse error / non-numeric `port` field)
- Birthday-collision invariant: deterministic fallback has 55,535 distinct ports; first collision expected at ~√55535 ≈ 236 projects on a shared host; second project silently falls back via `EADDRINUSE` retry in tmux server bootstrap — acceptable for single-developer/single-host target
- Wave structure: T1 types.ts → T2/T3/T4 three new classes (parallel) → T5 integration.ts rewrite → T6 fork-bridge removal + 3 consumer files → T7 3 vitest + 6 BATS → T8 atomic commit + VERIFICATION.md
- types.ts uses ORIGIN: ≥2 OR "no ORIGIN needed" justification — used 2 ORIGIN annotations
- Each new class requires `// ORIGIN: opencode-tmux/src/<filename>.ts:<line>` ≥3 annotations
- `SessionManagerAdapter` (renamed from `ForkSessionManagerAdapter`) has 6 methods: `onSessionCreated` + `respawnIfKnown` (from SessionManager); `getMainPaneId` (from TmuxMultiplexer, PROMOTED from private) + `sendKeys` + `listPanes` (from TmuxMultiplexer); `createPaneGridPlanner: (debounceMs?: number) => PaneGridPlanner`
- CORRECTED signatures (post-verify in Wave 3): `getMainPaneId: () => Promise<string | null>` (not sync `string | undefined`); `createPaneGridPlanner: (debounceMs?: number) => PaneGridPlanner` (not no-arg)
